key_insight: Tandem SAW and twin-ICE cold-wire SAW can weld HSLA tubular steels while lowering effective heat input and refining the weld/HAZ structure, especially when cold-wire ratio is optimized.
engineering_relevance: The study points to process settings that can improve bead geometry, hardness response, and joint integrity for thicker HSLA steel plates in industrial tubular fabrication.
method: Compared tandem arc SAW with twin-ICE SAW at different cold-wire feeding ratios
Used optical/SEM microscopy, stereoscopy, and microhardness mapping
evidence_signal: The abstract reports narrower HAZ, grain refinement, more acicular ferrite, and hardness changes consistent with reduced effective heat input, with complete joint penetration achieved in optimized cases.
watch_item: Excessive cold-wire addition can reduce penetration depth, so process windows must be tuned to avoid incomplete fusion or loss of CJP.
paper_title: Tandem and twin submerged-arc welding of high-strength, low-alloyed structural steels for tubular applications
